openQuestions: Is the mode_matrix.default_policy enum exhaustive, or do speckit/* and remaining deep/* introduce a fifth policy?
Does the route-manifest topology need its own blocking core beyond OWNED ASSETS + PRESENTATION BOUNDARY?
Are any deep/* inlined autonomous-directive fragments genuinely render contracts rather than display content?

resolvedQuestions.text: RQ1 — Workflow-YAML canonical schema + corpus divergence
RQ3 — Mode completeness check + mode matrix
RQ4 — Route-manifest YAMLs + executable-edge cycle parsing

keyFindings.text: AL4 PROVEN (not hypothesized): extractCommandTargets runs COMMAND_TARGET raw-text regex with no comment stripping; comment lines _routes.yaml:5,240 and create_readme_auto.yaml:37 / create_readme_confirm.yaml:9,40 match it; the create/readme.md <-> create_readme_auto.yaml:37 pair fires a false CMD-S3-ROUTE-CYCLE P0 today
Workflow-YAML schema is observable + stable (confirm==auto+checkpoints invariant) but absent from canon AND validator - the strongest layer has no machine contract

Adapter checks reachability only, never completeness: no checkModeCompleteness; inspectCommandSurface flags missing files, never missing mode declarations; corpus accidentally complete (28 :auto routers, 28 _auto.yaml)
OWNED ASSETS table split 20 (2-col | Purpose | Asset |) vs 15 (3-col | Asset | Path | Purpose |); create family disagrees with the create-command canon's own template inside the strongest layer

doctor route-manifest (_routes.yaml schema_version + routes[] + mcp_subroutes[] + standalone[] + 13 per-route YAML) is a distinct route-manifest argv-router topology the canon misclassifies as direct-dispatch/No-workflow-YAML; also not harvested by either advisor

memory/search inline render envelope is a legitimate render-fidelity exception (machine-emit, deterministic) captured only by prose 'hard render reminder'; needs typed presentation.exceptions[] so an intent-aware leak check can sanction it without an allowlist

AL6 'wrong .txt ownership labels' is the visible symptom of triple-path drift (path hand-copied into OWNED ASSETS + EXECUTION TARGETS + PRESENTATION BOUNDARY); fix = single typed presentation.owner + projection invariant, no per-command relabeling

deep/research.md is a fat router (184 lines vs create/command.md 54 for same topology): inlines a human-audience display box (DIRECT INVOCATION REQUIRED) + autonomous directive that its OWN PRESENTATION BOUNDARY says belongs in the .txt/YAML - a leak, not a sanctioned exception

ruledOutDirections: Rewrite the YAMLs to a new schema (triad is validated-good; capture existing schema)
Hardcode an allowlist of inline-tolerant commands (preserves regeneration defect; rots)
Force every router to support both :auto and :confirm (contradicts legitimate zero/single-mode topologies)
Strip all # globally before extraction (too blunt; corrupts code anchors/prose)
Slim deep/* routers by hand before the contract (012 A7 anti-pattern)
